sections.paragraph:
m. [Root בָּרַר].-

(1) purity. Commonly with the addition of יָדַיִם Psa 18:21, 25 Psa 18:25, or כַּפַּיִם Job 9:30 22:30 , cleanness of hands, being put figuratively for innocency. Once בֹּר simply is used in the same sense, 2Sa 22:25.

(2) that which has a cleansing property: lixivium, alkali, i.q. בֹּרִית which see; Job 9:30. Alkali was used by the ancients for washing, when mixed with oil instead of soap, and also in smelting metals that they might melt the more quickly, Isa 1:25.
